heading: Example
body:
Consider the following encryption hierarchy for a three-node replica set.
Atlas uses the CMK (customer-managed key) from Google Cloud KMS to encrypt a unique
MongoDB Master Key for each node in the cluster. Each node also contains
three databases, each of which is encrypted with a unique
per-database encryption key. When the cluster starts up,
Atlas decrypts the MongoDB Master Key by using the CMK (customer-managed key) from
Google Cloud KMS and supplies this to the MongoDB Server.

> **Note:** If you revoke Atlas's access to the CMK (customer-managed key), Atlas shuts down the
> nodes in your cluster and you can't access your data until
> you restore access to the CMK (customer-managed key).

![Diagram of CMK workflow with AKV and Atlas](/images/azure-kms-cmk-diagram.png)
